title: caleya major
intro: Caleya major, also known as the "Mountain Cedar" or "Yellow Cedar," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Caleya major include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Caleya major include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote digestive health. Caleya major is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
